findings.evidence: <main> at line 27 contains another <main> at line 31. Only one <main> landmark is valid per document.

findings.description: Ensure the main landmark is at top level
findings.evidence:
Fix any of the following:
  The main landmark is contained in another landmark. [<main>]

findings.description: Ensure the document has at most one main landmark
findings.evidence:
Fix any of the following:
  Document has more than one main landmark [<main>]

Fix any of the following:
  The landmark must have a unique aria-label, aria-labelledby, or title to make landmarks distinguishable [<main>]

findings.description: The PDF uses a clear two-column layout for Receipts and Disbursements, with FY 2020-2021 data on the left and Duration of Program data on the right. The HTML converts this into a single four-column table, collapsing the two distinct side-by-side sections into one merged table structure.
findings.evidence: PDF shows two visually separate sections side by side (FY 2020-2021 on left, Duration of Program on right) with their own headers. HTML merges them into a single table with four columns.

findings.description: In the PDF, dash entries (indicating zero or no value) for Receipts items like Rebate and Interest are shown as '-'. In the HTML, these are rendered as '$ -' which is slightly different formatting but preserves the meaning.
findings.evidence: PDF: Rebate: $ - (dash), Interest: $ - (dash). HTML: '$ -' for same fields.

findings.description: The Fund Balance and Encumbered in Contracts summary section appears as standalone right-aligned lines in the PDF, but is rendered as a separate table in the HTML. While data is preserved, the visual grouping differs from the original.
findings.evidence: PDF shows 'Fund Balance as of 3/31/2021: $ 13,054,308.66' and 'Encumbered in Contracts as of 3/31/21: $ 9,051,143.57' as right-aligned standalone lines. HTML renders them in a separate 'Summary Metric' table.

findings.description: The DSCA Site Statistics section in the PDF uses a simple list format with values aligned to the right. The HTML converts this into a formal table with headers 'Statistic' and 'Value', which adds structure not present in the original.
findings.evidence: PDF: simple indented list with values. HTML: formal table with column headers 'Statistic' and 'Value'.
